trigger: 30-minute MVP builder run: add Google Drive-backed evidence file upload + access-controlled streaming download routes with integration tests and audit coverage.
scope: Implement a multipart evidence file upload endpoint that writes evidence_artifacts.drive_file_id via the Google Drive API, plus an access-controlled streaming download endpoint that proxies Drive alt=media responses without exposing storage IDs; cover missing config/creds, token exchange failure, provider failure, and success via integration tests with fetch stubs.
changes: Extended functions/_lib/google-drive.ts with Drive multipart upload and alt=media download helpers built on the service-account JWT token exchange access token.

Added functions/api/submissions/[id]/evidence/upload.ts implementing a student-scoped multipart upload flow that enforces max size, uploads bytes to Drive under the evidence root folder, writes evidence_artifacts.drive_file_id, and audits success and failure states without leaking storage IDs.

Added functions/api/evidence/[id]/download.ts implementing an access-controlled streaming download that enforces canAccessStudent, audits download attempts/failures, and proxies Drive content bytes with no-store headers.
Updated functions/_lib/workflow.ts to use explicit .ts import specifiers so the Node test runner can execute integration tests against code paths using workflow helpers.
Kept functions/api/evidence/[id]/check-access.ts free of storage-id fields while reporting fileBytesReady for google_drive_file artifacts.
Added tests/evidence-drive-file.integration.test.mjs covering missing session/config, token exchange failure, Drive provider failures, and upload/download success behavior with audit assertions.
Updated docs/mvp-requirements-catalog.md, docs/automation-backlog.md, docs/automation-memory.md, and docs/progress/run-log.md to reflect MVP-013 Drive file upload/download progress.
known_limits: Upload endpoint enforces a 5MB limit for multipart uploads; larger files will require resumable Drive uploads.
Download endpoint uses files.get?alt=media which only applies to binary Drive files; Google Docs/Sheets exports require files.export handling.
Integration tests stub fetch() responses; a follow-on pass should verify upload/download against real Cloudflare Pages secrets and the configured Drive evidence root.
next_actions: Configure Cloudflare Pages secrets GOOGLE_DRIVE_CLIENT_EMAIL and GOOGLE_DRIVE_PRIVATE_KEY and verify /api/health shows googleDriveCredentialsConfigured=true.
Verify real Drive upload + download via the new endpoints in preview/production, then wire the alpha/student UI to call the upload endpoint.
Add resumable uploads for larger evidence and add export handling for Google Docs types when needed.
